model.name: DREAM S2 news-cycle retention
model.lambda_interpretation: lambda = elapsed hours since topic attention peak
model.signal_preprocessing: Formal S2 fit uses circadian-corrected publish-time article counts: raw count divided by expected global publishing/activity factor.
model.observation_clock: article.published_at, not GitHub Action run time
model.history_role: data/history.json retains raw article records and first_seen/last_seen provenance; publication time reconstructs the curve.
model.comparison: Delta AIC = AIC(exponential beta=1) - AIC(stretched S2 beta free)
model.cycle_archive_role: data/cycles.json stores completed formal cycle summaries so prior S2 learning remains visible when a new wave resets the current topic.

articles.title: Gunman who opened fire near White House was known to Secret Service
articles.summary: Suspect who died after exchanging fire with agents had tried to enter the complex last summer, records show A gunman who opened fire outside the White House on Saturday before he was shot by federal agents was already known to the US Secret Service, court records show. The man, 21, was taken to a nearby hospital, before he was later pronounced dead. He had previously tried to enter the complex, according to an affidavit filed in DC superior court in 2025, following an arrest nearby. Continue rea

articles.title: UK records heatwave as parts of England hit record highs for May
articles.summary: Santon Downham in Suffolk first area to hit threshold, recording temperatures of more than 27C for three consecutive days Parts of the UK are officially in a heatwave as temperatures soared to within reach of May records. The first area of the UK to hit the threshold was Santon Downham in Suffolk, which reached the criteria of recording temperatures of more than 27C for three consecutive days at 11.30am on Sunday. Continue reading...

articles.title: UK swelters as hottest May day for nearly 80 years hits
articles.summary: England, Wales and Northern Ireland hit record temperatures on Sunday, with Heathrow in London reaching 32.1C high England, Wales and Northern Ireland sweltered in record temperatures for 2026 on Sunday, which was also the UK’s hottest May day for at least 79 years. Heathrow in west London saw a high of 32.1C, while Cardiff recorded 27.4C and Armagh saw 23.4C. Continue reading...
